launch_bundle.topic: Decide whether to start product validation for an AI study product.

participants.responsibility: Compress the product into one sharp repeated experience.
Check validation discipline, downside, and kill rules.
Check whether the thinnest technical loop can support real use.

selected_panel.responsibility: Compress the product into one sharp repeated experience.
Check validation discipline, downside, and kill rules.
Check whether the thinnest technical loop can support real use.

agent_arguments.role_duty: Compress the product into one sharp repeated experience.
agent_arguments.core_conclusion: Do not start with a broad AI tutor; test one repeated study moment.
evidence: The room summary already converged on a narrow repeated study bottleneck.
A broad feature set would make early retention signals hard to interpret.
agent_arguments.biggest_problem: The first wedge is still not narrow enough to test cleanly.
agent_arguments.opposed_misjudgment: Treating a feature list as a product value proposition.
agent_arguments.concrete_recommendation: Pick one study bottleneck and test whether students repeat it for seven days.
uncertainties: Which study moment has the strongest combination of frequency and pain.

agent_arguments.role_duty: Check validation discipline, downside, and kill rules.
agent_arguments.core_conclusion: The next step is acceptable only if the kill rules are written before the test starts.
evidence: The room had an unresolved tension about weak signals and false validation.
Student curiosity can look like product demand during the first week.
agent_arguments.biggest_problem: There is not yet a hard stop condition for weak retention.
agent_arguments.opposed_misjudgment: Interpreting polite positive feedback as demand.
agent_arguments.concrete_recommendation: Define retention, completion, and active return thresholds before building more.
uncertainties: Whether the target group has enough repeated frequency to support retention.

agent_arguments.role_duty: Check whether the thinnest technical loop can support real use.
agent_arguments.core_conclusion: The technical loop can stay thin if quality control is explicit.
evidence: A single input-to-feedback loop can be tested without a full platform.
Manual review can reduce model-error exposure during the first validation run.
agent_arguments.biggest_problem: Too much automation would add noise before demand is proven.
agent_arguments.opposed_misjudgment: Assuming model capability means the product should expand scope immediately.
agent_arguments.concrete_recommendation: Use a semi-manual thin loop for the first cohort.
uncertainties: Which step should remain manual to protect feedback quality.

moderator_summary.topic_restatement: The debate is deciding whether to start formal product validation, not whether to build a full platform.

participants_and_roles.responsibility: Compress the product into one sharp repeated experience.
Check validation discipline, downside, and kill rules.
Check whether the thinnest technical loop can support real use.

consensus_points: Start with one study bottleneck, not a broad AI tutor.
Write kill rules before expanding scope.
Use a thin technical loop with quality control.
core_conflicts: The product argument wants a sharper experience while the validation argument wants harder stop rules.
hidden_assumptions: Students will submit enough real study material to make the test meaningful.
moderator_summary.preliminary_recommendation: Run a seven-day single-bottleneck validation with explicit retention and stop thresholds.
review_focus: Check whether the recommendation is concrete enough to execute.
Check whether the evidence separates facts, inferences, and uncertainties.

evidence_gaps: The exact first student segment still needs live discovery.
overlooked_issues: The first acquisition channel is not yet specified.
severe_red_flags: The recommendation depends on a fabricated live provider claim.
reviewer_result.rationale: Reject the result because a severe claim-boundary red flag invalidates the decision.

open_questions: Which student segment should be tested first?
Which acquisition channel can produce the first cohort?

facts: The result used a visible three-agent panel.
The reviewer allowed a final decision with score 8.
inferences: A narrower test will produce cleaner retention signals.
uncertainties: The highest-frequency study bottleneck is still unproven.
recommendations: Run one narrow validation before productizing.

notes: This fixture validates schema shape only.
It is not host-live validation and not provider-live validation.
